goal: Run per-row supervised live proofs broadly and promote every row that passes; keep everything else disarmed with exact reasons.

tooDangerous: cursor.invisible - the preview value itself removes cursor visibility
noRuntimeVerification: input.scroll_method - unset str options read back as [[EMPTY]], which cannot be reapplied; revert cannot restore the exact unset state (found by live proof)

passedProof.note: all 36 receipts (row, original, preview, fallback, env) are recorded in PROVEN_INPUT_ROWS and exported in the provenRows section of the proof-plan report

fallbackStrategies: cursor rows: keyboard and pointer events keep flowing; only cursor appearance/warp behavior changes
keyboard rows: pointer remains usable to click Revert/Cancel
touchpad/tablet/touch rows: external mice and keyboards are unaffected
focus rows: both devices keep working; keyboard navigation remains available
all rows: the dead-man timeout auto-revert fires without any user input
worst case: TTY (Ctrl+Alt+F3) rollback instruction with the recorded original value, or restart Hyprland; the config file is never modified
recoveryInstructions: every plan carries a row-specific recovery instruction combining the automatic countdown revert with the TTY rollback path
sourceGuards: the input proof module contains no command construction, no config writes, no reload path, and no active config references
the live harness is ignored by default and requires HYPRLAND_SETTINGS_RUN_INPUT_LIVE_PROOF=1 plus an explicit row id
the harness gate refuses unknown rows, non-input rows, rows without fallback, non-proof-ready rows, and already-proven rows
rows are promoted only via PROVEN_INPUT_ROWS receipts; the dead-man layer arms input/cursor rows only when a receipt exists (test-enforced)
UI proof copy is display-only: no hyprctl strings, no executor calls, no gate access from UI code
testsAdded: per_row_input_live_proof_applies_and_reverts (ignored, env-gated; run once for cursor.inactive_timeout: passed)
nextRecommendedWork: Remaining disarmed rows need hardware (18 touch-family) or a secondary-device supervised proof (left_handed, rotation, resolve_binds_by_sym); the structured-family active-config pilot still awaits its explicit user decision.
liveProofsRunDetail: batch one: 36 attempted, 27 passed, 9 failed (8 from the FiniteChoice int-quoting executor bug, 1 scroll_method [[EMPTY]] readback); executor fixed; batch two: 35 attempted, 35 passed; every failure restored or verified at its original/default value

rowsStillDisarmedDetail.touchFamily: 18 touchpad/tablet/touch rows: proving behavior requires the hardware present
heldBackByJudgment: input.left_handed - button swap could confuse the confirm click
input.rotation - pointer orientation change impairs aiming
input.resolve_binds_by_sym - the user's own keybinds could misfire

defectsFoundByLiveProofs: executor rendered FiniteChoice values as quoted strings, but int-typed choice options reject them (integer type requires a bool or an integer); fixed: numeric choices render bare and every choice value is validated against the row's allowed list
unset str options read back as [[EMPTY]] and cannot be round-tripped; scroll_method blocked with that reason
